title: Fear Garden — Level 4 Detective
description: LEVEL 4 DETECTIVE CASE version of Fear Garden with unique suspect, method, motive, evidence, and answers.

answerKey.method: Sabotaged the patio misting line and electrified a wet microphone stand
answerKey.motive: To silence Vivian before she revealed Darius sold private event footage to a rival promoter
answerKey.keyEvidence: Misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and darius’s booth automation log
answerKey.explanation: Darius Cole had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and Darius’s booth automation log with the failed statement: Darius claimed the booth software was offline.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Fear Garden begins at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This session is Level 4: Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Difficulty Loaded
publicClues.text: This is Level 4: Detective Case. Evidence and answers are unique to this level.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Answer the checkpoint after reviewing the body scene.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare the timeline before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Digital and venue systems now matter.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Find the motive evidence.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Vivian LeBlanc missed two calls from Darius Cole before the garden music cut out.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Vivian moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Vivian texted Darius: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Darius replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Vivian’s phone pinged near the patio garden path at 8:07 PM.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Darius’s device appears nearby during the same short window.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A Beer Garden event ledger shows a suspicious private payout connected to silence vivian before she revealed darius sold private event footage to a rival promoter.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout trail links to a device or account controlled by Darius.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A guest photo shows Darius near the restricted service path before the incident.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: Another photo captures the scene detail: misting valve opened seconds before Vivian grabbed the mic.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A patio livestream catches a missing gap just before Vivian disappears from frame.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The posted version removes the seconds that would show who crossed behind the garden wall.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Darius saved a contact under “Garden Services,” matching a suspicious payment line.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Vivian saved the same number with the note “ask about fake paperwork.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the killer needed venue access, timing, and a reason to silence Vivian.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the scene points to planning, not a random Beer Garden accident.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: A security export skips the exact Beer Garden patio window.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: File metadata connects the edit to the suspect’s access trail: Darius Cole.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A browser search asks how to remove timestamp metadata from venue clips.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search matches the method: sabotaged the patio misting line and electrified a wet microphone stand.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Fear Garden at Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. You are playing Level 4: Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 4: Detective Case. The culprit was Darius Cole. The method: Sabotaged the patio misting line and electrified a wet microphone stand. The motive: To silence Vivian before she revealed Darius sold private event footage to a rival promoter. The proof: Misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and darius’s booth automation log.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: What makes Vivian’s death suspicious?

options.label: misting valve opened seconds before Vivian grabbed the mic
A normal spilled pint near the stage
A table number written backward
A guest closing a patio umbrella

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which timeline clue matters most?

options.label: The DJ restarted a playlist
Darius’s booth automation log controlled the patio cue
A guest paid with cash
A server dropped a tray outside

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which digital clue suggests planning?

options.label: A low battery warning
A misspelled hashtag
a video-sale invoice matches the rival promoter
A normal Wi-Fi reconnect

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 4: What clue points toward motive?

options.label: A birthday receipt
A lost wristband
An ordinary tab transfer
guest footage shows the mic stand moved after setup

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which statement begins to collapse?

options.label: Darius claimed the booth software was offline
Maya said the plants were watered
Janelle said the gate was busy
Darius said the speaker was loud
Maya Bell
Darius Cole
Trevor Knox
Janelle Price

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Poisoned a shared punch bowl
Cut the stage lights only
Sabotaged the patio misting line and electrified a wet microphone stand
Staged a random parking-lot fall
To win a trivia prize
To protect a karaoke score
To hide a family recipe
To silence Vivian before she revealed Darius sold private event footage to a rival promoter

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: Misting valve timer, video-sale invoice, and darius’s booth automation log
A guest rumor and a napkin
A broken patio chair only
A single missed call with no context

questions.prompt: Final 5: Which suspect statement falls apart?

options.label: Maya said the music was loud
Darius claimed the booth software was offline
Trevor said the patio was crowded
Janelle said the menu was changed
